# Test fixtures for `platform/mtls/piv`

All certificates in this directory are **synthetic** — generated by the
recipe below. They mimic the PIV/CAC shape (Microsoft UPN OtherName SAN,
DoD assurance-level policy OID, `clientAuth` EKU) but are signed by a
locally-generated test root, NOT by any real DoD certificate authority.

**Do NOT commit real DoD-issued certificates to this repository.**

## Fixture inventory

### TRD 03-03 (PIV — UPN + chain validation)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sample_piv_root.pem` | Synthetic trust anchor that signs all `sample_piv_*` leaf certs. |
| `sample_piv_root.key.pem` | Private key for the trust anchor (test-only). |
| `sample_piv_cert.pem` | Valid leaf: UPN `john.doe.1234567890@mil`, policy `2.16.840.1.101.3.2.1.3.7`, `clientAuth` EKU, `notAfter = now + 10 years`. |
| `sample_piv_cert.key.pem` | Private key for the valid leaf (test-only). |
| `sample_piv_no_upn.pem` | Leaf signed by the same root, but with an `rfc822Name` SAN only — no OtherName UPN. Used to assert `ErrNoUPN`. |
| `sample_piv_no_upn.key.pem` | Private key for the no-UPN leaf (test-only). |
| `sample_piv_expired.pem` | Leaf signed by the same root with `notAfter` in the past (1990). Used to assert `ErrChainExpired`. |
| `sample_piv_expired.key.pem` | Private key for the expired leaf (test-only). |
| `sample_piv_other_root.pem` | A completely unrelated self-signed CA. Used to assert `ErrUntrustedChain` when the leaf does NOT chain to it. |
| `sample_piv_other_root.key.pem` | Private key for the unrelated root (test-only). |

### TRD 07-01 (CAC — EDIPI + FASC-N + trust store loader)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sample_cac_root.pem` | Synthetic DoD-shaped test root that signs `sample_cac_cert.pem`. Generated by `gen_cac.go`. |
| `sample_cac_root.key.pem` | Private key for the CAC test root (test-only). |
| `sample_cac_cert.pem` | DoD-CAC-shaped leaf: UPN OtherName `1234567890@mil` + FASC-N OtherName (16-byte OCTET STRING `D013CE4F8210D8B612835866E7EE99F4`) + `clientAuth` EKU. Used by `ExtractEDIPI` and `ExtractFASC_N` happy-path tests. |
| `sample_cac_cert.key.pem` | Private key for the CAC leaf (test-only). |
| `dod_truststore_test.pem` | PEM-bundle concatenation of `sample_cac_root.pem` + `sample_piv_root.pem`. Used by `LoadDoDTrustStore` happy-path test. |

**Why a Go generator** instead of openssl for the CAC fixture: OpenSSL's
`x509v3_config` `OtherName:` syntax cannot express a raw OCTET STRING
value for FASC-N — it only supports the typed inner-value variants
(`UTF8`, `IA5`, `INTEGER`, etc.). Hand-assembling the SAN extension
with `crypto/x509` + `encoding/asn1` produces deterministic, byte-stable
fixtures. The recipe lives at `testdata/gen_cac.go` (excluded from the
build via `//go:build ignore`).

**ASN.1 gotcha**: `asn1.Marshal` of a struct with
`Value asn1.RawValue \`asn1:"explicit,tag:0"\`` does **not** add the
[0] EXPLICIT wrapper around the raw value (silent skip — Go quirk). For
UTF8String inner values use `Value string \`asn1:"explicit,tag:0,utf8"\``
(typed field — Go honours the explicit tag). For OCTET STRING inner
values hand-assemble the EXPLICIT wrap. The generator demonstrates
both.

## Regenerating the fixtures

Requires **OpenSSL ≥ 1.1.1** for `-addext` and named OID support. macOS
default LibreSSL works for the basic CSR/cert flow but not for the
`OtherName:1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3;UTF8:...` SAN syntax — use Homebrew's
`openssl@3` if on macOS:

```bash
OPENSSL=${OPENSSL:-openssl}   # or /opt/homebrew/opt/openssl@3/bin/openssl
```

Run from inside this `testdata/` directory. Regenerate every five years
or whenever the `notAfter` clock approaches the present (current valid
fixtures expire ten years after generation).

### 1. Trust anchor (the "DoD-shaped" test root)

```bash
$OPENSSL req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes \
  -keyout sample_piv_root.key.pem \
  -out    sample_piv_root.pem \
  -subj   "/CN=Test PIV Root CA" \
  -days   3650 \
  -sha256
```

### 2. Valid PIV leaf with UPN + policy

```bash
cat > /tmp/piv_san.cnf <<'EOF'
basicConstraints      = CA:FALSE
keyUsage              = digitalSignature
extendedKeyUsage      = clientAuth
subjectAltName        = otherName:1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3;UTF8:john.doe.1234567890@mil
certificatePolicies   = 2.16.840.1.101.3.2.1.3.7
EOF

$OPENSSL req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes \
  -keyout sample_piv_cert.key.pem \
  -out    /tmp/sample_piv_cert.csr \
  -subj   "/CN=John Doe"

$OPENSSL x509 -req \
  -in        /tmp/sample_piv_cert.csr \
  -CA        sample_piv_root.pem \
  -CAkey     sample_piv_root.key.pem \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out       sample_piv_cert.pem \
  -days      3650 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile   /tmp/piv_san.cnf
```

### 3. Leaf with no UPN (rfc822Name SAN only)

```bash
cat > /tmp/piv_no_upn.cnf <<'EOF'
basicConstraints = CA:FALSE
keyUsage         = digitalSignature
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
subjectAltName   = email:no.upn@example.test
EOF

$OPENSSL req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes \
  -keyout sample_piv_no_upn.key.pem \
  -out    /tmp/sample_piv_no_upn.csr \
  -subj   "/CN=No UPN"

$OPENSSL x509 -req \
  -in        /tmp/sample_piv_no_upn.csr \
  -CA        sample_piv_root.pem \
  -CAkey     sample_piv_root.key.pem \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out       sample_piv_no_upn.pem \
  -days      3650 \
  -sha256 \
  -extfile   /tmp/piv_no_upn.cnf
```

### 4. Expired leaf

OpenSSL `x509 -req` does not accept negative `-days`. Use the
`ca`-style flow or — simpler — generate a self-signed cert with a
back-dated validity window via `req -x509` and a custom `-set_serial`
date. Easiest: use Go's `crypto/x509` from a tiny generator script.
This repo's fixture is generated by `gen_expired.go` (committed under
`testdata/` as documentation; not part of the build):

```go
//go:build ignore
package main
// see ./gen_expired.go
```

Run once via `go run ./testdata/gen_expired.go` from the package dir.

### 5. Unrelated root (for ErrUntrustedChain)

```bash
$OPENSSL req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes \
  -keyout sample_piv_other_root.key.pem \
  -out    sample_piv_other_root.pem \
  -subj   "/CN=Unrelated Test Root" \
  -days   3650 \
  -sha256
```

### 6. CAC-shaped fixtures (UPN + FASC-N OtherNames)

The CAC leaf fixture (`sample_cac_cert.pem`) needs a SubjectAltName
that carries **two** OtherName entries — one UPN (UTF8String) and one
FASC-N (OCTET STRING). OpenSSL's config-file `subjectAltName` directive
can express the UPN form but not the OCTET STRING form, so the fixture
is generated by a Go program instead. Run from the package directory
(one level up from `testdata/`):

```bash
cd ..
go run ./testdata/gen_cac.go
```

That writes:

- `testdata/sample_cac_root.pem` + `.key.pem` (self-signed root)
- `testdata/sample_cac_cert.pem` + `.key.pem` (leaf with UPN + FASC-N SANs)
- `testdata/dod_truststore_test.pem` (concatenation of CAC root + PIV root)

To extend the CAC fixture set (e.g., to add a CAC cert with **no** UPN —
exercising the FASC-N-only fallback path), edit `gen_cac.go` and add a
new template stanza. Do NOT introduce a parallel openssl recipe for
CAC-shaped certs; the inability to encode an OCTET STRING OtherName via
the config file is the root reason `gen_cac.go` exists.

The `.key.pem` files are committed for fixture reproducibility but
carry test-only private keys with no security value (synthetic root
signing synthetic leaves). They MUST NOT be reused outside this
package.

## Verification after regen

```bash
# PIV fixtures
$OPENSSL x509 -in sample_piv_cert.pem -noout -text | grep -E "(UPN|1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3|Policy)"
$OPENSSL verify -CAfile sample_piv_root.pem sample_piv_cert.pem  # expect: OK

# CAC fixtures
$OPENSSL x509 -in sample_cac_cert.pem -noout -text \
  | grep -E "(1234567890@mil|1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3|2.16.840.1.101.3.6.6|UPN)"
$OPENSSL verify -CAfile sample_cac_root.pem sample_cac_cert.pem  # expect: OK
```

Then run `go test ./platform/mtls/piv -count=1 -v -race` from the repo root.
